author: @Gwydion67
text:
Late congrats to the baby, its parents and grandparents! 🎉👶🤝

Talking about the risks and other downsides of your journey due to the pandemic, including the reasons for being careful, shows your brave attitude.
Especially when expecting at least some critical comments, also.
Apart from a handful of committed haters (probably some of decent political incentive), most people just lack the essential details for correcting their perspective. Which isn't as easy as one may think, cause of all those manyfold aspects and variables to consider. 🤯
Not to mention the emotional stress. Which you *did* mention. 😉

Again, chapeau and a big thank you! 🎩👍

author: @MrHunterseeker
text:
youtube is censoring any comments talking about this subject. I had 2 deleted so far, with no offensive language, only talking about the sickness. 

(The comment that got censored:)

This sounds so convoluted. I am so glad I am in the good ol' US of A, where I can get up, and just decide to travel to another state, city, or zip code and just jump in my car and drive there without any hassle whatsoever as long as we wear a mask.  


I have not had any (medicine), haven't took any (you know whats), because the (you know what) is untested and not food and drug admin approved and they have no idea what the side effects will be years down the road. I am pretty sure I had the (you know what) though back when it first blew through the US before anyone knew what it was 2 years ago. I was sick and  that only lasted a few days but it permanently screwed my lungs up and for the past 2 years I have been trying to recover from it.  I remember when I was sick with (you know what) that at one point my lungs didn't feel like they were working and it felt like they were filled with fluid. Like I was drowning. If I wouldn't have been healthy, or would have been a bit older at the time I don't think I would have survived that event. The reason I say this, is I live a pretty active lifestyle, from the work I do to the hobbies I keep. Doing regular yard work was nothing for me until after I had the (you know what), that first summer after,  it was a struggle to even hold a weed eater long enough to do a few feet of trimming. Packing in firewood was the same, no trouble before the (you know what), but after, I would load up some wood and have to take a break. I really noticed how down I was that first summer after being sick.  Now it's been 2 years since I had it, and I have exercised and worked every day to get my lungs/body back into a decent shape, and I have to tell you, This stuff isn't to be messed with. I still refuse to take the (you know what) because- when has the government ever worked in my best interest? When have they not ever lied to me? When you have government officials telling you to do something like this your best bet is to do the complete opposite because they have figured out something you don't know and have monetized it already.

author: @HumilityThroughSilence
text:
Congratulations on the new edition to the family. 
I'm taken back by this absolutely disproportionate reaction to something which is reasonably low risk.
Correct me if I'm wrong, but this is an interspecies (able to transmit from various animals) virus which is also in aerosol format.
So, yes people may not have it in their body, BUT that by no stretch of the imagination denotes that they aren't in fact carriers of it.
I'll be going out on new year's eve, in order for me to gain entry to the events I wish to enter, I'll have to provide a negative LFT (lateral flow test) result.
What strikes me as insanity is people who've had just 2 jabs simply have to show a QR code to gain entry to the exact same events.
So somehow this QR code is capable of stopping the virus from landing about their person and preventing transmission of the virus, which again is also in aerosol form, and to quote our very own government "anyone can get it and anyone can spread it"
Now, I'm not in the least bit against people who wish to be jabbed, it's entirely their God given right to choose what they wish to have put in their bodies, but I'm confused as to how any of them genuinely believe that it can in fact reduce transmission or infections when the data both here in the UK and indeed in nations across Europe with far greater restrictions are in fact seeing increases in both infections and hospitalisations.
How do we address the interspecies transmission?
How many people have even investigated how this came about originally ie gain of function?
Last but not least, when do we accept that we can not stop it or even slow it down and just get on with our lives?
